app.title: IR / RAG Evaluation Lab
app.subtitle: Information retrieval and RAG evaluation toolbox
query.placeholder: Enter a query, e.g. why use BM25 baseline
analytics.title: Evaluation Analytics
analytics.pairwiseHint: Select two experiments to inspect win/loss/tie queries, rank deltas, latency deltas, and relevant hit differences.
textMining.title: Text Mining
textMining.networkWorkbenchHint: Tune nodes, edges, communities, and layout to explore the corpus term network like Gephi.
textMining.selectNodeHint: Click a node in the network to inspect details.
llm.noStoredDiagnosis: No stored local LLM diagnosis yet
llm.assistiveNotice: Local LLM judge is an assistive signal, not ground truth.
llm.rewriteExperimentHint: Compare original, lexical, semantic, entity, and domain rewrites across multiple queries.
workflow.title: Experiment Workflow
jobs.title: Job Status
tour.step.overview.title: Start from the Evaluation Command Center
tour.step.overview.body: This control-room view summarizes the active dataset, best retriever, failure risk, and LLM judge status so reviewers see the whole lab first.
tour.step.overview.action: Check the dataset and best Recall@10, then continue into the experiment workflow.
tour.step.dataset.title: Switch reproducible datasets
tour.step.dataset.body: Every page follows the dataset selector, including corpus, metrics, bad cases, RAG, and text mining analysis.
tour.step.dataset.action: Choose sample data or upload custom JSONL, then return to analytics to reproduce results.
tour.step.journey.title: Understand how data is processed
tour.step.journey.body: The Data Journey page visualizes JSONL validation, indexing, retrieval, evaluation, and reporting instead of showing only final numbers.
tour.step.journey.action: Switch the tabs below to inspect each stage, effect, and destination page.
tour.step.upload.title: Upload custom documents and queries
tour.step.upload.body: Upload documents.jsonl and queries.jsonl. Without an upload, the lab still demonstrates the full pipeline with the sample dataset.
tour.step.upload.action: After upload, the dataset selector moves to the new dataset and later experiments use it.
tour.step.corpus.title: Inspect corpus quality
tour.step.corpus.body: Quality checks surface empty text, duplicate docs, missing labels, and qrels status. This is the first step toward credible benchmarks.
tour.step.corpus.action: Confirm document count, query count, qrels, and quality warnings before experiments.
tour.step.workflow.title: Configure multiple retrievers
tour.step.workflow.body: BM25, Dense, Hybrid, and Rerank share one workflow with K values, alpha, and dense backend controls.
tour.step.workflow.action: Run all writes to DuckDB and creates the data needed for metrics, bad cases, and reports.
tour.step.query.title: Single-query diagnostics
tour.step.query.body: Query Evaluator compares BM25, Dense, Hybrid, and Rerank results, score breakdowns, and query rewrite variants.
tour.step.query.action: Enter a query or pick an existing one, then run all modes to inspect ranking differences.
tour.step.comparison.title: Compare experiment runs
tour.step.comparison.body: This page compares Recall@K, nDCG, MRR, latency, and zero-result rate with charts and tables.
tour.step.comparison.action: Select multiple runs and inspect best/worst badges plus exportable comparison data.
tour.step.analytics.title: Interactive failure heatmaps
tour.step.analytics.body: Evaluation Analytics connects metric matrices, failure heatmaps, rank movement, and retriever battle views.
tour.step.analytics.action: Click a heatmap cell or battle row to drill into a single query.
tour.step.diagnostics.title: Query diagnostics drill-down
tour.step.diagnostics.body: This view shows query text, relevant docs, retriever ranking lanes, missed docs, first relevant rank, and LLM diagnosis.
tour.step.diagnostics.action: Use it to explain why a query failed instead of reporting only averages.
tour.step.rag.title: RAG citation workbench
tour.step.rag.body: The RAG page splits answers into claims and maps them to evidence, citation coverage, and local LLM faithfulness judgments.
tour.step.rag.action: Run a query, then inspect unsupported claims and whether citations actually support the answer.
tour.step.badcases.title: Review bad cases
tour.step.badcases.body: Bad Case Viewer supports root cause, severity, owner, status, and AI suggestions for a practical reviewer workflow.
tour.step.badcases.action: Filter high severity or open cases, then open the drawer to inspect expected vs retrieved rankings.
tour.step.llm.title: Local LLM evaluation dashboard
tour.step.llm.body: This page tracks llama.cpp judge history, success rate, invalid JSON, confidence, and latency.
tour.step.llm.action: Verify real connection or fallback mode, and treat judge output as an assistive signal.
tour.step.textmining.title: Text mining and network analysis
tour.step.textmining.body: Co-occurrence networks, collocations, Sankey, association rules, and community detection add interpretability beyond IR metrics.
tour.step.textmining.action: Expand the Gephi-like network to inspect term communities and failure relationships.
tour.step.report.title: Deliver reproducible reports
tour.step.report.body: Experiment runs and reports combine metrics, bad cases, dataset cards, LLM stats, and text mining charts into a benchmark artifact.
tour.step.report.action: Export CSV/JSON or run make report to produce a reproducible interview demo.
journey.title: Follow data from JSONL to retrieval evaluation, RAG citation, and text mining
journey.subtitle: This is not a chat UI. It is a reproducible evaluation workflow. Upload data or use the sample dataset, then watch it move through cleaning, indexing, retrieval, comparison, and explanation.
journey.upload.title: Dataset upload
journey.upload.help: After custom JSONL upload, the backend validates schema, writes to DuckDB, updates the dataset registry, and lets every page use the same dataset.
journey.upload.sampleFallback: Without uploaded data, the guide uses the sample dataset to demonstrate the full flow.
journey.tabs.title: Step-by-step pipeline tabs
journey.tabs.help: Move through each stage to understand the real effect and analytical meaning.
journey.stage.raw.title: Raw JSONL standardization
journey.stage.raw.meaning: documents.jsonl and queries.jsonl are normalized into one schema so BEIR, MS MARCO, OpenAlex, and custom corpora can enter the same evaluation flow.
journey.stage.raw.effectA: Validate doc_id, title, text, and metadata fields.
journey.stage.raw.effectC: Preserve license and source information without redistributing restricted raw corpora.
journey.stage.clean.title: Validation, deduplication, and quality checks
journey.stage.clean.meaning: Data quality directly affects Recall and nDCG. This stage finds empty text, duplicate docs, orphan qrels, and missing labels.
journey.stage.clean.effectA: Reduce false bad cases caused by bad data.
journey.stage.clean.effectB: Make dataset cards explicit about limitations.
journey.stage.clean.effectC: Persist quality checks to DuckDB for traceable reports.
journey.stage.index.title: BM25, Dense, and Hybrid indexes
journey.stage.index.meaning: BM25 provides the deterministic lexical baseline, Dense can use sentence-transformers, and Hybrid combines both signals with alpha.
journey.stage.index.effectA: BM25 becomes the baseline for every advanced retriever.
journey.stage.index.effectB: Dense retrieval has deterministic fallback when a model is unavailable.
journey.stage.index.effectC: Hybrid alpha is controlled by experiments and the frontend.
journey.stage.retrieve.title: Querying, ranking, and score breakdown
journey.stage.retrieve.meaning: Each search creates rankings, score breakdowns, and latency so lexical, semantic, hybrid, and rerank behavior can be compared.
journey.stage.retrieve.effectA: Compare ranking changes for the same query across retrievers.
journey.stage.retrieve.effectB: Use score bars to explain BM25, dense, and hybrid contributions.
journey.stage.retrieve.effectC: Persist run records for comparison and reporting.
journey.stage.metrics.title: Retrieval metrics and per-query analytics
journey.stage.metrics.meaning: Precision@K, Recall@K, MRR, MAP, nDCG@K, latency, and zero-result rate are analyzed globally and per query.
journey.stage.metrics.effectA: Avoid judging retrievers with a single average score.
journey.stage.metrics.effectB: Support heatmap, scatter, and rank movement drill-downs.
journey.stage.metrics.effectC: Locate first relevant rank and missed relevant docs.
journey.stage.compare.title: Experiment comparison
journey.stage.compare.meaning: Multiple experiment runs can be compared by win/loss/tie, rank delta, latency delta, and best/worst retriever.
journey.stage.compare.effectA: Answer whether Hybrid really improves BM25 or Dense.
journey.stage.compare.effectB: Use battle matrices to show retriever matchups.
journey.stage.compare.effectC: Export CSV/JSON for delivery and reproducibility.
journey.stage.badcases.title: Bad case root cause analysis
journey.stage.badcases.meaning: The lab classifies missed relevant docs, ranked-too-low cases, zero results, and BM25 vs Dense disagreement, then lets reviewers add root cause and status.
journey.stage.badcases.effectA: Turn failures into a trackable review workflow.
journey.stage.badcases.effectB: Use AI suggestions while keeping human review.
journey.stage.rag.title: RAG citation and faithfulness
journey.stage.rag.meaning: Answers are split into claims, mapped to evidence and citations, and judged by local LLM as supported, partially supported, unsupported, or contradictory.
journey.stage.rag.effectA: Citation coverage is not enough; answer support rate also matters.
journey.stage.rag.effectC: LLM judge records latency, confidence, and prompt type.
journey.stage.textmining.title: Text mining network analysis
journey.stage.textmining.meaning: Co-occurrence networks, collocations, association rules, Sankey charts, and community detection make corpus features and failure patterns easier to explain.
journey.stage.textmining.effectA: Use network graphs to find core term communities.
journey.stage.textmining.effectB: Use Sankey links to connect categories and failure causes.
journey.stage.textmining.effectC: Use basket analysis to find frequent term groups.
journey.stage.llm.title: Local llama.cpp evaluation signals
journey.stage.llm.meaning: llama.cpp is used for evaluation support, not chat: claim support, failure explanation, query rewrite, and insight narratives.
journey.stage.llm.effectA: Track success rate, invalid JSON, and latency.
journey.stage.llm.effectB: Present confidence and rationale as product UI.
journey.stage.llm.effectC: Label it clearly as an assistive signal, not ground truth.
